questions.question: In the question below is given a statement followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. You have to assume everything in the statement to be true, then consider the two conclusions together and decide which of them logically follows beyond a reasonable doubt from the information given in the statement. Statements: Prime age school-going children in urban India have now become avid as well as more regular viewers of television, even in households without a TV. As a result there has been an alarming decline in the extent of readership of newspapers. Conclusions: 1. Method of increasing the readership of newspapers should be devised. 2. A team of experts should be sent to other countries to study the impact of TV. on the readership of newspapers.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Neither of them follows. The statement concentrates on the increasing viewership of TV. and does not stress either on increasing the readership of newspapers or making studies regarding the same. So, neither I nor II follows.

questions.question: Select the combination of letters that when sequentially placed in the blanks of the given series will complete the series. rtx_sx_z_txy_ _yz
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. yyrsx. The given series is rtx_sx_z_txy_ _yz. To find the pattern, you can divide the series into two equal blocks of 8 letters each: rtxysxyz / rtxysxyz

questions.question: A, P, R, X, S and Z are sitting in a row. S and Z are in the centre. A and P are at the ends. R sits immediate left of A. Who is to the right of P?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. X. The seating arrangement is as follows: ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/11.png) Therefore, the right of P is X.

questions.question: At a party, the number of girls is half the number of boys. After an hour, five boys leave the party and three girls join the party and the number of boys and girls are now equal. How many people were present at the party an hour before?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 24. B : G 2 : 1 2G = B (G + 3) = (B − 5) G + 3 = 2G − 5 8 = G B = 16 Before 1 hour, number of persons in the party = G + B = 8 + 16 = 24

questions.question: A father was twelve times as old as his son twenty years ago. Now he is twice as old as his son. What is the present age of the son?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 22. Son Father Present age x 2x A.T.Q. ⇒ 2x – 20 = 12 (x -20) x = 22 ∴ son = 22 yrs. Father = 44 yrs.

questions.question: Which number will replace the question mark (?) in the following series? 2, 5, 11, 23, 44, ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 77. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_06-02-2025_D9.png)

questions.question: A junior team member has brilliant ideas but is too nervous to speak during group meetings. How can you encourage them?
options: Privately praise their ideas and invite them to share in a supportive setting
Take credit for their ideas to boost your own profile
Ignore them until they find their own courage
Put them on the spot by asking them to present without warning
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Privately praise their ideas and invite them to share in a supportive setting. Private encouragement builds confidence without creating public pressure or embarrassment.

questions.question: Which option address is EXACT match of the master address? Master Address: H.No. 2-3-45/A, Sai Nagar Colony, Opp. Apollo Hospital, Hyderabad - 500034
options: H.No. 2-3-45/A, Sai Nagar Colony, Opp. Apollo Hospital, Hyderabad - 500034
H.No. 2-3-45/A, Sai Nagar Colony, Opp, Apollo Hospital, Hyderabad - 500034
H. No. 2-3-45/A, Sai Nagar Colony, Opp. Apollo Hospital, Hyderabad - 500034
H.No. 2-3-45/A, Sai Nagar, Opp. Apollo Hospital, Hyderabad - 500034
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. H.No. 2-3-45/A, Sai Nagar Colony, Opp. Apollo Hospital, Hyderabad - 500034. Option A matches every character, punctuation and spacing exactly.

questions.question: If CAB is coded as 6 and BED is coded as 40, then how will HAD be coded as?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 32. C A B = 3 × 1 × 2 = 6 B E D = 2 × 5 × 4 = 40 H A D = 8 × 1 × 4 = 32

questions.question: If Leo gave away 5 marbles, he would have exactly half as many as Sarah. However, if Sarah gave Leo 2 marbles, they would both have the exact same number. How many marbles does Leo have?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 14. If Sarah gave Leo 2 marbles, they would have the same number." This means Sarah has 4 more marbles than Leo (S = L + 4) If Leo gave away 5 marbles, he would have half as many as Sarah." L - 5 = S/2 Substitute S = L + 4 into the second equation: L - 5 = (L + 4)/2 Multiply everything by 2 to clear the fraction: 2L - 10 = L + 4 Subtract Lfrom both sides and add 10 to both sides: L = 14 Leo has 14 marbles (and Sarah has 18).

questions.question: The clock in my lounge room is 10 minutes slower than the clock on my phone, which is 6 minutes slow. My tram always leaves 6 minutes early, although it is scheduled for 8:55am. It takes me 20 minutes to get to the tram stop. What time must I leave, according to my lounge room clock, in order to catch my tram?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. None of the above. Earlier than 8:13 am Tram leaves 8:49, which is 6 mins earlier than it is supposed to go 8:49 minus 20 minutes’ walk is 8:29 8:29 - 6 mins slower on phone and - 10 minutes slower in the lounge room, 8:29 minus 6minutes minus 10 minutes is 8:13 am

questions.question: Direction: A word is mentioned only by a group of numbers, as shown in one of the options. The number groups in the options are mentioned by two classes of letters as shown in Matrices given below. The no. of rows & columns in Matrix I are from 0- 4 & that of in Matrix II are 5-9. A letter can be deﬁned using the no. of row & then the no. column. e.g. ‘A’ can be deﬁned by 00, 12, 23 etc. & ‘P’ by 58, 69, 75 etc. Find the code/group of numbers for the given word. Matrix -I ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/BTCj31770366403620.png) Matrix - II ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/BTCj31770366403621.png) PAST
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 86, 12, 31, 76. To solve this, we need to decode the coordinates provided in the options. Remember the rule: the first digit is the Row and the second digit is the Column. PAST → 86, 12, 31, 76

questions.question: Study the given pattern carefully and select the number that can replace the question mark (?) in it. 10 4 14 35 15 3 5 25 14 7 6 12 18 ? 8 16
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 9. Divide the first number with the second number then multiply result with the third number. I. 10 ÷ 4 × 14 = 35 II. 15 ÷ 3 × 5 = 25 III. 14 ÷ 7 × 6 = 12 IV. 18 ÷ ? × 8 = 16 ⇒ ? = 144 ÷ 16 = 9

questions.question: A said to B: "The woman I met in the park yesterday was the only daughter of your brother's father." How is that woman related to B?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Sister. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/13.png) Since the woman is the only daughter of B's father, she is B's sister.

questions.question: If 18α12 = 3, 2α14 = -6 and 4α4 = 0, then find the value of 2α16 = ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. -7. The pattern for the operation ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/BTCj31770366403610.gif) is to subtract the second number from the first, and then divide the result by 2. 18α12 = 3 → (18 - 12) ÷ 2 = 3 2α14 = -6 → (2 - 14) ÷ 2 = -6 4α4 = 0 → (4 - 4) ÷ 2 = 0 Then, the value of 2α16 = → (2 - 16) ÷ 2 = -7

questions.question: A piece of paper is folded and punched as shown below in the question figures. From the given answer figures, indicate how it will appear when opened. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/BTCj31770366403600.png)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 3. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/BTCj31770366403606.png)

questions.question: In following question some statements followed by some conclusions are given. Taking the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts, read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusion logically follows the given statements. Statements : I. All girls are mad II. Some boys are mad Conclusions : I. Some mad are girls II. All boys are girls
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Only conclusion I follows. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/BTCj31770366403595.png)

questions.question: If a mirror is placed on the line AB, then which of the answer figure is the right image of the given figure? ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/BTCj31770366403586.png)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. C. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/12.png)

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd letter/letters from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. GR. All are pairs of opposite letters except for GR.

questions.question: Arrange the given words in the sequence in which they occur in the dictionary. 1. Read 2. Real 3. Ready 4. Rather 5. Ratify
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 45132 4. Rather 5. Ratify 1. Read 3. Ready 2. Real

questions.question: Select the number pair in which the two numbers are related in the same way as the two numbers in the given pair. 65 : 126
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 9 : 28. 65 : 126 = (4)³ + 1 : (4 + 1)³ + 1 Similarly, 9 : 28 = (2)³ + 1 : (2 + 1)³ + 1

questions.question: Here are some words translated from an artificial language. gemolinea means fair warning gerimitu means report card gilageri means weather report Which word could mean “fair weather?”
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. gemogila. geri means report, mitu means card, And gila means weather. The word for the code gemo and linea is not determined; we can't find the exact code for Fair. But among all the options gila code is used only for option 1. Thus, gemogila is the correct choice.

questions.question: In a row of girls, Nithya and Suganya occupy ninth place from the right end and tenth place from the left end, respectively. If they interchange their places, then Nithya and Suganya occupy seventeenth place from the right and eighteenth place from the left respectively. How many girls are there in the row?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 26. Since Nithya & Suganya exchange places, so, Nithya’s new position is the same as Suganya’s earlier position. Nithya’s position is 17th from the right and 10th from the left in the current arrangement. Therefore, Number of girls in the row = (17 + 10 - 1) = 26.

questions.question: Select the related word from the given options: Justice : Fairness :: Liberty : ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Emancipation. This question uses a Synonym Relationship. Justice : Fairness — Justice and Fairness are synonyms; they both refer to the quality of being equitable and unbiased. Liberty : Emancipation — Similarly, Liberty refers to the state of being free. Among the options, Emancipation (the act of being set free from legal, social, or political restrictions) is its closest synonym.

questions.question: The series below contains a sequence of numbers. Identify the incorrect combination accordingly. 4, 5, 12, 39, 163
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 163. The sequence follows a pattern where each term is multiplied by a progressively increasing integer ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/BTCj31770366403565.gif) and then the same integer is added: 4 × 1 + 1 = 5 5 × 2 + 2 = 12 12 × 3 + 3 = 39 39 × 4 + 4 = 160 (Instead of 163) The correct sequence is 4, 5, 12, 39, 160.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor formally introduced the doctrine of "Sulh-i-Kul" as state policy?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Akbar. Emperor Akbar (reigned 1556–1605) was the one who formally adopted this as a state policy."Sulh-i-Kul", meaning "peace with all", was a doctrine of universal tolerance and secular governance. It emphasized: • Equal respect for all religions • Non-discrimination in administration • Promotion of harmony in a multi-religious empire

questions.question: Match the following: Articles Related to A) 339 1) Appointment of a Commission to investigate the conditions of Backward Classes B) 340 2) Definition of Scheduled Castes C) 341 3) Definition of Scheduled Tribes D) 342 4) Control of the Union over the welfare of SCs and STs
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. A – 4, B – 1, C – 2, D – 3. Article 339: Union government controls welfare of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes. Article 340: President can appoint a commission to study Backward Classes. Article 342: President defines which groups are Scheduled Tribes. Article 341: President defines which groups are Scheduled Castes.

questions.question: Which planet takes the longest time to complete one revolution around the Sun?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Neptune. Neptune takes the longest time to complete one revolution around the Sun — about 165 Earth years. Jupiter: ~12 years Saturn: ~29.5 years Uranus: ~84 years Neptune: ~165 years

questions.question: What is Kanthasth 3.0, which was launched at the 2025 Regional Official Language Conference?
options: A mobile app for learning Hindi songs
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. A software that translates. Kanthasth 3.0 is a computer software made to help translate text between Hindi, English, and many other Indian languages.It was developed by C-DAC Pune and launched in 2025 at a government event called the Regional Official Language Conferenceheld in Guwahati.

questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly describes the role of “Sthanakas” in Indian classical dance forms?
options: Sthanakas are static standing postures symbolizing divine or heroic characters in performances.
Sthanakas are fast rhythmic foot movements used to build energy and momentum.
Sthanakas are vocal chants or mantras that accompany slow and static dance sequences.
Sthanakas are traditional costume elements worn by dancers during classical performances.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Sthanakas are static standing postures symbolizing divine or heroic characters in performances. Option 1 correctly defines Sthanakas. They are static standing postures that dancers hold during a performance. These poses often represent gods, heroes, or important characters, helping convey the story through visual expression. Option 2 describes fast foot movements, which are different from Sthanakas. Sthanakas are not movements but poses. So, this is incorrect. Option 3 talks about vocal chants or mantras, which are unrelated to the physical poses that Sthanakas refer to. Option 4 mentions costumes, which again has no connection with the concept of Sthanakas.

questions.question: ______ is the mechanical transfer of energy to a system of from a system by an external force on it.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Work. Work is defined in physics as the mechanical transfer of energy that occurs when a force is applied to an object and the object moves in the direction of the force. If energy is transferred to a system, work is positive. If energy is transferred from a system, work is negative.

questions.question: Which Viceroy of India presided over the Bhutan War (1864–1865), culminating in the Treaty of Sinchula that expanded British influence in the Eastern Himalayas?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Lord Lawrence. The Bhutan War (1864–1865) occurred during Lord Lawrence’s tenure as Viceroy (1864–1869). The war ended with the Treaty of Sinchula, under which Bhutan ceded some territories to the British, increasing their influence in the Eastern Himalayas.

questions.question: Which of the following options correctly describes how the unemployment rate is calculated in India?
options: Percentage of employed persons in the population
Percentage of unemployed persons among persons in the labour force
Percentage of unemployed persons in the total population
Percentage of persons in the labour force
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Percentage of unemployed persons among persons in the labour force. The unemployment rate is a key labor market indicator—it shows how many people who are willing and able to work are not actually employed. But importantly, it focuses only on the labour force, not the entire population.

questions.question: Which of the following statements related to renal physiology is/are correct? • Sodium reabsorption in the ascending limb of the loop of Henle occurs actively and helps create a hyperosmotic medullary interstitium. • The presence of aquaporin channels in the collecting duct is regulated by the secretion of aldosterone. • Glucose reabsorption in the nephron depends on the availability of specific transport proteins, and once these are saturated, glucose appears in the urine. • The descending limb of the loop of Henle is impermeable to water but permits active sodium reabsorption.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 1 and 3 only. Statement 1 – Correct: Active sodium transport in the ascending limb helps generate a hyperosmotic medullary gradient, crucial for water reabsorption. Statement 2 – Incorrect: Aquaporins in the collecting duct are regulated by ADH, not aldosterone. Aldosterone primarily affects sodium reabsorption in the DCT and collecting duct. Statement 3 – Correct: Glucose reabsorption is via SGLT transporters in the PCT. Once the transport maximum (Tm) is exceeded (like in diabetes mellitus), glucose spills into urine. Statement 4 – Incorrect: The descending limb is permeable to water, but impermeable to ions; the opposite is true for the ascending limb.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the sources of the Indian Constitution is correct?
options: Fundamental Rights were inspired by the British Constitution, and Fundamental Duties were borrowed from the US Constitution.
Fundamental Rights were borrowed from the US Constitution, the parliamentary system from the British Constitution.
The parliamentary system was adopted from the US Constitution, and Fundamental Duties were inspired by the Soviet Constitution.
The entire Constitution is based solely on the British Constitution.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Fundamental Rights were borrowed from the US Constitution, the parliamentary system from the British Constitution. Option 2 is correct because: Fundamental Rights → borrowed from the US Constitution Parliamentary system → adopted from the British Constitution Fundamental Duties → inspired by the Soviet Constitution

questions.question: Match the following: Sport Award Awardee A) Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna 2025 1) Banu Mushtaq B) Padma Shri 2025 2) Harmanpreet Singh C) International Booker Prize 2025 3) Harvinder Singh D) Dronacharya Awards 2024 4) Deepali Deshpande
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. A – 2, B – 3, C – 1, D – 4. A) Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna: This is India’s highest sporting honor. In 2025, it was awarded to Harmanpreet Singh for his outstanding performance in hockey. B) Padma Shri 2025: Padma Shri is the fourth highest civilian award in India.It was given to Harvinder Singh, a Paralympic archer, for his excellent achievements. C) International Booker Prize 2025: “Heart Lamp,” a short story collection by Banu Mushtaq, translated into English by Deepa Bhasthi, became the first Kannada work to win the 2025 International Booker Prize, marking a historic moment for Indian regional literature. D) Dronacharya Awards 2024: This award is for coaches who have produced outstanding sportspersons. It was awarded to Deepali Deshpande (Shooting coach), in 2024.

questions.question: Which government department is responsible for conducting the Census of India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Ministry of Home Affairs. The Census of India is conducted by the Office of the Registrar General and Census Commissioner, which operates under the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA). The MHA oversees the entire census process because it involves extensive data collection related to the population, which is crucial for governance, planning, and resource allocation.

questions.question: What does ‘p’ stand for in ‘pH’?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Potenz. The "p" in pH stands for "Potenz", which is a German word meaning power or potential. The term pH was introduced by Danish chemist Soren Sorensen in 1909, and it stands for "power of hydrogen" or "potential of hydrogen" — referring to the concentration of hydrogen ions (H⁺) in a solution.

questions.question: Which committee emphasized the need for people’s participation and democratic decentralization for rural development in India by reviewing and reforming Panchayati Raj?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Ashok Mehta Committee. The Ashok Mehta Committee (1977-78) was set up to review the working of Panchayati Raj institutions and emphasized greater people’s participation and democratic decentralization for effective rural development. Confusion: The Balwantrai Mehta Committee (1957) recommended the establishment of the Panchayati Raj system but was more focused on administrative decentralization rather than participatory reform.

questions.question: In the context of India's monetary transmission mechanism, consider the following statements: • A reduction in the policy repo rate by the RBI always leads to a proportional reduction in lending rates by commercial banks. • The effectiveness of monetary transmission in India is influenced by factors such as the extent of market competition, the level of Non-Performing Assets (NPAs), and the dominance of fixed-rate loans. • Transmission of monetary policy is rarely faster through bond markets than through traditional banking channels. • The Marginal Cost of Funds-based Lending Rate (MCLR) regime was introduced to strengthen the pass-through of policy rates to retail borrowers. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 2 and 4 only. Statement 1 – Incorrect: Repo rate cuts don’t always lead to lower lending rates due to weak transmission, high NPAs, and rigid deposit rates. Statement 2 – Correct: Transmission is affected by NPAs, market competition, and dominance of fixed-rate loans. Statement 3 – Incorrect: Bond markets respond faster than banks as they are more market-sensitive. Statement 4 – Correct: MCLR was introduced in 2016 to improve the pass-through of policy rates to borrowers.

questions.question: At the Republic Day Parade 2026, which among the following Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) or other auxiliary forces was awarded the Best Marching Contingent?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF). The results of the ‘Popular Choice’ category based on the online poll conducted on the MyGov portal for the citizens to vote for their favourite Tableau and Marching Contingents are as follows: Best Marching Contingent among three Services - Assam Regiment Best Marching Contingent among CAPFs/other auxiliary Forces – CRPF Top three tableau (States/UTs): • Gujarat (Mantra of Swadeshi – Self-Reliance – Freedom: Vande Mataram) • Uttar Pradesh (Culture of Bundelkhand) • Rajasthan (Golden Touch of the Desert: Bikaner Gold Art (Usta Art) Best tableau from Central Ministries/Departments – Deptt. of School Education & Literacy (National Education Policy 2020: Rocketing Indian School Education on the Path to Viksit Bharat).

questions.question: Which disease results from the insufficient secretion of insulin hormone by the pancreas?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Diabetes mellitus. The hormone insulin is produced by the beta cells of the pancreas. It plays a crucial role in regulating blood sugar levels by helping cells absorb glucose from the bloodstream for energy or storage.When the pancreas produces insufficient insulin, glucose cannot enter the cells efficiently, causing high blood sugar levels.This condition is known as Diabetes Mellitus.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Non-Cooperation Movement (1920–1922) is correct?
options: It was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in response to the JallianwalaBagh massacre.
It called for complete independence from British rule immediately.
It urged Indians to boycott foreign goods, schools, and British institutions.
It ended with the signing of the Gandhi-Irwin Pact.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. It urged Indians to boycott foreign goods, schools, and British institutions. The Non-Cooperation Movement was a mass protest launched by Mahatma Gandhi and the Indian National Congress against British colonial rule. It aimed to resist British authority through non-violent means by boycotting British goods, educational institutions, courts, and government services. Confusion: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre (1919) created widespread anger, but the main triggers for the Non-Cooperation Movement were the Rowlatt Act (1919) and the Khilafat Movement. The movement started in 1920, a year after the massacre, so it wasn’t solely a direct response to it.

questions.question: Tiruchirappalli (Trichy) in Tamil Nadu is located on the banks of which river?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Kaveri. Tiruchirappalli, also known as Trichy, is a major city in Tamil Nadu situated on the banks of the Kaveri (Cauvery) River. The Kaveri is one of the longest rivers in southern India, flowing through Karnataka and Tamil Nadu before emptying into the Bay of Bengal. It is an important source of water for irrigation, drinking, and hydroelectric power in the region.

questions.question: Which of the following compounds does not undergo nucleophilic substitution easily due to resonance stabilization of the leaving group?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Vinyl chloride. Vinyl chloride has a chlorine atom attached to a carbon that's part of a double bond. Because of this double bond, the molecule becomes more stable and doesn't easily let the chlorine go. That’s why it doesn’t react easily in substitution reactions. The others are more reactive because they don’t have this kind of stability.

questions.question: Where is the headquarters of the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) located?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Geneva, Switzerland. The headquarters of the ITU is located in Geneva, Switzerland. The International Telecommunication Union (ITU) is a specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for issues related to information and communication technologies (ICT). It coordinates the global use of the radio spectrum, develops technical standards, and works to improve telecommunication infrastructure worldwide.

questions.question: Which of the following Articles is related to the Ancillary powers of Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Article 140. Article 140 of the Indian Constitution deals with the ancillary powers of the Supreme Court. It empowers Parliament to confer additional powers on the Supreme Court as may be necessary to enable it to perform its functions effectively.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about India’s physiographic regions is INCORRECT? • The Vindhya Range marks the traditional boundary between North India and the Peninsular Plateau. • The Thar Desert lies to the east of the Aravalli Hills. • The Ghats are Western older than the Eastern Ghats and exhibit more geological fragmentation. • The Shivalik Hills are the oldest mountain ranges in the Himalayan system.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Statement 2, 3 and 4 only. Statement 1: Correct — Vindhyas separate North India and Peninsular Plateau. Statement 2: Incorrect — Thar Desert lies west, not east, of Aravalli Hills. Statement 3: Incorrect — Eastern Ghats are older and more fragmented than Western Ghats. Statement 4: Incorrect — Shivalik Hills are the youngest, not oldest, Himalayan range.

questions.question: In the context of the Union Budget 2026–27, the Kartavya Framework is based on three key pillars. Which of the following does not form part of these pillars?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Environmental Preservation and Wildlife Protection. The Kartavya Framework is designed to transform India into a developed economy (Viksit Bharat) by 2047. It centers on the "three duties" (Kartavyas) of the state: driving long-term economic momentum, empowering human capital, and ensuring the "last mile" reach of development. The Union Budget 2026–27 introduced the three pillars of the Kartavya Framework, which are: Sustain Economic Growth: Accelerating/Sustaining Economic Growth is the First Kartavya which focuses on enhancing productivity, competitiveness, and building resilience to global dynamics. Fulfill Aspirations and Build Capacity: Fulfilling Aspirations & Building Capacity is the Second Kartavya which aims to build the capacity of youth and citizens as partners in India's prosperity. Inclusive Development (Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas): Inclusive Development (or Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas) is the Third Kartavya which ensures every family, community, and region has access to resources and opportunities.

questions.question: Which of the following Harappan sites is thought to have direct sea trade links with Mesopotamia?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Lothal. Lothal, located in present-day Gujarat, was a major Harappan port city with a well-planned dockyard. It is believed to have had direct sea trade links with Mesopotamia, making it one of the earliest known maritime trade centers in the ancient world.

questions.question: A dealer sells his goods at 10% loss on the cost price, but he uses a weight 25% less than the actual weight. Find his actual profit or loss percentage?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 20% Profit. CP : SP 10 : 9 3 : 4 30 : 36 = 5 : 6 Hence, Profit % = 20%

questions.question: A right-angled triangle PQR is inscribed in a circle whose diameter is 12 cm. An altitude QS is drawn from the right-angled vertex Q to the hypotenuse PR. If the length of the leg PQ = 8 cm, what is the length of the segment PS?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 5.33 cm. A right-angled triangle inscribed in a circle has its hypotenuse equal to the diameter of that circle. Given diameter = 12 cm. Therefore, the hypotenuse PR = 12 cm. In a right-angle triangle PQ² = PS × PR ⇒ 8² = PS × 12 ⇒ PS = 64/12 = 5.33 cm

questions.question: \({1\over{1+{1\over1+{1\over1 +{2\over x}}}}} ={5\over8}\), then what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 4. \({1\over{1+{1\over1+{1\over1 +{2\over x}}}}} ={5\over8}\) ⇒ \({1\over{1+{1\over1+{x\over x+2}}}} ={5\over8}\) ⇒ \({1\over{1+{x+2\over2x+2}}} ={5\over8}\) ⇒ \({2x+2\over3x+4}={5\over8}\) ⇒ 16x + 16 = 15x + 20 ⇒ x = 4

questions.question: The angle of depression to the foot of a building from the top of a \(93\sqrt3\) m high tower is 60°. How far is the tower located from the building?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 93 m. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Picture133.png) Let the foot of the building be at point ‘C’ and Tower (AB) = \(93\sqrt3\) m In ∆ABC, tan60° = \(AB\over BC\) ⇒ \(\sqrt3 = {93\sqrt3\over BC}\) ⇒ BC = 93 m

questions.question: An express train of length 480m passes through a tunnel of length 1200m in 48 seconds. What is the speed of the train in km/hr?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 126. Total distance = 1200 + 480 = 1680 m Time = 48 seconds Speed = \(1680\over48\) = 35 m/sec ⇒ 35 × \(18\over5\) = 126 km/hr

questions.question: A solid sphere is placed inside a cube such that it touches all six faces of the cube. What percentage of the cube’s volume is occupied by the sphere?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 52.38%. Let side of cube = a Sphere touches all faces, so diameter = side Radius = a/2 Volume of cube = a³ Volume of sphere = \(4π\over3\) × \(({a\over2})^3\) = \(πa^3\over6\) Percentage of cube occupied = \(π \over6\) × 100 ≈ 52.38%

questions.question: If x⁴ + 1 = 6x², find the value of \({x^2+1+3\sqrt2x\over x}\over{x+{{3x-1}\over x}}\).
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. \(\sqrt2\). x⁴ + 1 = 6x² ⇒ x² + \(1\over x^2\) = 6 ⇒ (x + \(1\over x\))² - 2 = 6 ⇒ x + \(1\over x\) = \(2\sqrt2\) ⇒ x - \(1\over x\) = \(\sqrt{(2\sqrt2)^2-4}\) = 2 So, \({x^2+1+3\sqrt2x\over x}\over{x+{{3x-1}\over x}}\) = \({x+{1\over x} +3\sqrt2}\over {x+3-{1\over x}}\) = \(2\sqrt2+3\sqrt2\over2+3\) = \(5\sqrt2\over5\) = \(\sqrt2\)

questions.question: If 40% of a number is less than its 60% by 30, then 30% of that number is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 45. Let the number be x 60% of x - 40% of x = 30 ⇒ 20% of x = 30 ⇒ x = 150 ⇒ 30% of x = 150 × 30% = 45

questions.question: If l, b & p are the length, breadth, and perimeter of a rectangle, and b, l & p are in Geometric Progression, then find the value of \(l\over b\)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. (\(\sqrt3\) + 1) : 1. Perimeter of the rectangle (p) = 2(l + b) And, given that b, l & p are in G.P.; i.e. l² = bp ⇒ l² = 2b(l + b) ⇒ l² = 2bl + 2b² ⇒ l² – 2bl + b² = 3b² ⇒ (l - b)² = 3b² ⇒ l - b = \(\sqrt3\)b ⇒ \(l\over b\) = \(\sqrt3+1\over1\) = (\(\sqrt3\) + 1) : 1

questions.question: The ratio of age of Sumit and Abhishek is 4 : 5. Five years earlier, the ratio was 7 : 9. Find the present ages of Sumit and Abhishek?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 40 years and 50 years. Sumit : Abhishek 5 Years Earlier (7 : 9) × 1 → (7 : 9) At Present (4 : 5)×2 → (8 : 10) 1 → 5 Hence, Sumit’s Present Age = 8 × 5 = 40 years And, Abhishek’s Present Age = 10 × 5 = 50 years

questions.question: If the external angle of a regular polygon is 24°, find the number of diagonals of the polygon.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 90. x = \(360\over24\) = 15 Number of diaonals = \(n(n-3)\over2\) ⇒ \(15\times12\over2\) = 90

questions.question: Find the value of the expression: 37– [45 – {67 – (24 – 56 – 16)}]

A juice vendor mixes water with pure orange juice. If he sells the mixture at the cost price of pure orange juice and makes a profit of \(33\frac{1}{3}\)%, what is the ratio of pure orange juice to water in the mixture, respectively?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 3 : 1. Since the profit earned is \(33\frac{1}{3}\)%, which is obviously because of added water to the pure orange juice; i.e., ratio of pure orange juice to the water = 3 : 1

questions.question: In the right-angled ∆ABC, right-angled at B, ‘D’ and ‘E’ are the midpoints of sides AB and BC respectively. If AE and CD are joined, AC = 26 cm, BC = 24 cm. Find the ratio of sides AE and CD?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. \(2\sqrt{61} : \sqrt{601}\). ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Capture.PNG) From the figure, AB = \(\sqrt{26^2-24^2}\) = 10 cm Now, AE = \(\sqrt{AB^2+BE^2}\) = \(\sqrt{10^2+12^2}\) = \(2\sqrt61\) cm And, CD = \(\sqrt{BC^2+BD^2}\) = \(\sqrt{24^2+5^2}\) = \(\sqrt{601}\) cm Hence, AE : CD = \(2\sqrt{61} : \sqrt{601}\)

questions.question: A computer is sold for Rs. 96,875, allowing a discount of 38%. What is the marked price of the computer [in ₹]?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 1,56,250. 38% discount 62% → ₹96,875 100% → ₹1,56,250

questions.question: The base of a prism is a rectangle whose length is twice its width. The perimeter of the base is 36 cm. The prism’s height decreases in an arithmetic progression from 16 cm in the first layer to 10 cm in the fourth layer. What is the total volume of the prism?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 3744 cm³. Given: Perimeter of base = 36 cm Let width = x, length = 2x ⇒ 2(l + w) = 36 ⇒ 2(2x + x) = 36 ⇒ 6x = 36 ⇒ x = 6 Width = 6 cm, Length = 12 cm Base area = 12 × 6 = 72 cm² Heights form an AP: 16, 14, 12, 10 Sum of heights => S = n/2 (a + l) = 4/2 (16 + 10) = 52 Total Volume = 72 × 52 = 3744 cm³

questions.question: In a race of 240m, A defeats B by 18m or 4 sec. Find the time taken by B to cover the entire length of the race. (answer to nearest integer)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 53 sec. A defeats B by 18 m or 4 sec in a 240 m race. This means B covers 18 m in 4 sec. Speed of B = 18/4 m/s Time taken by B to complete the race = 240/(18/4) = 160/3 sec = 53.33 sec ≈ 53 sec (nearest integer)

questions.question: If a line intersects the x-axis at (4, 0) and the y-axis at (0, −6), what is the slope of the line?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 3/2. Points given: (4, 0) and (0, −6) Slope m = (y₂ − y₁)/(x₂ − x₁) ⇒ m = (−6 − 0)/(0 − 4) ⇒ m = −6/−4 ⇒ m = 3/2

questions.question: An educational fund of ₹80,00,000 earns simple interest at 8% per annum. This annual interest is used to grant three scholarships. If the first and third scholarships are ₹2,00,000 and ₹1,40,000 respectively, what is the value of the second scholarship?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. ₹3,00,000. Fund = ₹80,00,000 Interest = 8% per annum Annual Interest = 8% × 80,00,000 = ₹6,40,000 First scholarship = ₹2,00,000 Third scholarship = ₹1,40,000 Second scholarship: 6,40,000 − (2,00,000 + 1,40,000) = ₹3,00,000

questions.question: A rectangle with a semi-perimeter of 130 cm has sides in the ratio 8:5. What is the length of the diagonal of a square that has the same area as that of the rectangle?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. \(40\sqrt5\). Side ratio:- 8 : 5 ⇒ 13 → 130 ⇒ 1→10 Length = 80 , Breadth = 50 Area = 80 × 50 = 4000 Square side ⇒ x ⇒ x² = 4000 ⇒ x = 20\(\sqrt10\) Diagonal - \(\sqrt2\)x ⇒ 20\(\sqrt20\) ⇒ 40\(\sqrt5\)

questions.question: A is thrice as good a workman as B, and together they can finish a work in 36 days. In how many days will B alone complete the same work?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 144 days. Total work → {3 + 1} × 36 → 144 B → \(144\over1\) → 144 days

questions.question: Rs. 8000 is lent at the rate of 16% pa on compound interest {compound quarterly}. What will be the compound interest of 6 months {in Rs.}?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 652.8. 16% p.a. ⟶ \(16\over4\) = 4% quarterly Effective CI% for 6 months = 8.16% {4+4 successive} Compound interest = 8.16% × 8000 = Rs. 652.8

questions.question: A startup has three stakeholders. A brought 50% of the capital, and B brought the rest. C joined later and was promised 20% of the total profit for branding. If the total profit was ₹2,50,000, how much was B’s share?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. ₹1,00,000. Total profit = ₹2,50,000 C’s share = 20% C’s profit = 20% × 2,50,000 = 50,000 Remaining profit = 2,50,000 − 50,000 = ₹2,00,000 A : B capital = 50% : 50% B’s share = ₹2,00,000/2 = ₹1,00,000

questions.question: The average monthly income of Ram is ₹12,000, and that of his brother Krishna is ₹8,000. Find the combined yearly income of both Ram and Krishna (in ₹).
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 2,40,000. Σ = 12000 × 12 + 8000 × 12 = ₹240000

questions.question: How many 6-digit numbers can be formed that are divisible by 9 and have ‘6’ as the first digit and ‘3’ as the third digit?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 1112. Number Formation: 6_ 3_ _ _ For 9’s divisibility, we need the sum of all digits to be divisible by 9. We may take any digit from 0 to 9 in the blank place. Possibilities of blank places as: 6 0 3 0 ­ 0 0, 6 0 3 0 0 9, 6 0 3 0 ­1 8, 6 0 3 0 ­2 7, …………….., 6 9 3 9 ­9 9 It means numbers from 0000, 0009, 0018 to 9999. Total numbers = \(9999\over9\) + 1 = 1112 Hence, a total of 1112 such numbers are possible

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Vacillate. The correct spelling is -Vacillate Exultent - Exultant - उल्लासपूर्ण - Feeling or expressing great happiness or triumph Example: The crowd was exultant after their team won the championship. Vacillate - Vacillate - हिचकिचाहट - To hesitate or waver in one's decisions or opinions Example: She vacillated between two job offers. Aerobecs - Aerobics - एरोबिक्स - A form of exercise that improves cardiovascular health Example: Aerobics is a popular form of exercise among fitness enthusiasts. Lesiure - Leisure - अवकाश - Free time or relaxation Example: She enjoys reading in her leisure time.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The new school was built in close __________ to the residential area, making it convenient for students to walk there.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Proximity. The word proximity means nearness in space, time, or relationship. In this context, it describes the school being physically close to the residential area, which makes it easy and convenient for students to walk there. Other Options: • Propriety– शिष्टाचार / मर्यादा – Refers to correct or appropriate behaviour or conduct; it has nothing to do with physical location. Example: “He always behaved with the utmost propriety at work.” • Remote – दूरस्थ / दूरका – Means something far away in distance or time. This is the opposite of what the sentence suggests. Example: “The village is in a remote area with little access to roads.” • Peripheral – परिधीय / गौण – Refers to something on the edge or not central. While it may sound similar, it doesn’t fit the idea of closeness or central location. Example: “His contributions were peripheral to the main discussion.” • Proximity – निकटता / समीपता – Refers to the state of being near in space or relationship. Example: “The proximity of the school to the residential area reduced travel time.”

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no correction is needed, select ‘No correction required’. She is one of the few candidates that has consistently met all the eligibility criteria.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. that have consistently met. ❌ Error Concept: Subject-Verb Agreement with Relative Pronouns [from the chapter: Subject-Verb Concord] 📚 Grammatical Concept: When a relative pronoun such as “who,” “which,” or “that” is used, the verb following it must agree in number with its antecedent [the noun it refers to]. • In this sentence, the relative pronoun “that” refers to “candidates” — a plural noun. • Therefore, the verb should also be plural [have, not has]. 🔧 How to improve it: Replace “that has” with “that have” to correctly match the plural antecedent “candidates.” ✔ Correct Sentence: She is one of the few candidates that have consistently met all the eligibility criteria. It can be said that option 3 is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the following sentence. Before entering the lab filled with nonsense chemicals, the researchers wore the requested safety gear to protect themselves from harmful exposure.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Noxious, Requisite. The sentence describes researchers entering a lab filled with harmful substances and wearing protective equipment — indicating a need for words related to toxic exposure and mandatory safety requirements. The original words “nonsense” and “requested” are incorrect or unrelated in meaning. Let’s see the meaning of all the key words: • Noxious – विषैला / हानिकारक – Harmful or poisonous, especially relating to fumes or chemicals. Example: “The noxious gases from the factory forced residents to evacuate.” • Requisite – अनिवार्य / आवश्यक – Required or necessary for a specific purpose. Example: “She submitted all the requisite documents before the deadline.” • Poisonous – विषैला – Typically refers to something that causes harm when consumed or absorbed, like plants or food. Example: “These berries are highly poisonous if eaten.” • Demanding – कठिन / थकानेवाला – Used to describe people or tasks that require a lot of effort, not equipment. Example: “This project is very demanding and time-consuming.” • Harmful – हानिकारक – A general word for causing damage or injury; not as specific as “noxious.” Example: “Harmful UV rays can damage the skin.” • Optional – वैकल्पिक – Not required; something that can be skipped. Example: “Wearing a tie to the event is optional.” • Requested – अनुरोधित – Something that has been asked for, not necessarily mandatory. Example: “He requested a day off.” • Nonsense – बकवास / निरर्थक – Has no meaning in this context; unrelated to chemicals. Example: “That theory is complete nonsense.” ✅So, Option [3] Noxious, Requisite best fits the meaning and tone of the sentence.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. fell by the workers. ❌ Error Concept: Incorrect Verb Usage [from the chapter: Transitive and Intransitive Verbs – Fall vs. Fell] 🔍 Grammatical Concept: The verb "fall" is intransitive, meaning it does not take a direct object [e.g., The tree fell]. The verb "fell" [as a transitive verb] means to cut down and requires a direct object [e.g., The workers felled the tree]. In this sentence, the action was done by workers, so the verb must be transitive, which means "fell" is incorrect here—it should be "felled." 🔧 How to correct it: Replace “fell” with “was felled” to form the correct passive voice with the transitive verb. ✔ Correct Sentence: The old oak tree was felled by the workers during the storm last night. It can be said that option 2 is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the grammatically correct sentence.
options: The employees must to follow the safety guidelines strictly.
She must submits the assignment before noon.
They must following the instructions carefully.
The candidate must carry a valid identity proof.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. The candidate must carry a valid identity proof. • CONCEPT – Use of Modal Verb “Must” (Base Verb Rule) ✅ • “Must” is a modal verb which is used to express compulsion, necessity, obligation or strong requirement. • All modal verbs (must, can, could, should, may, might, will, would, shall) are always followed by the base form (V1) of the verb. • After a modal verb, we never use “to + verb”, and we also never use verb forms ending in -s / -es / -ing / -ed. • The basic structure remains: • Subject + modal verb + base verb (V1). • ✔ Hence, only the sentence that correctly follows • Subject + must + base verb (V1) • without any grammatical violation is Option (4). Correct Sentence – “The candidate must carry a valid identity proof.” ✅ WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG ❌ Option (1) is incorrect because the modal verb must is wrongly followed by “to follow”. After any modal verb, the verb should appear only in its base form (follow), not in the infinitive form. ❌ Option (2) is incorrect because “submits” is the third-person singular form of the verb. After a modal verb, the main verb never takes -s / -es, therefore the correct form should be submit. ❌ Option (3) is incorrect because “following” is the present participle (-ing form). Modal verbs cannot be followed by -ing form directly; they must be followed by the base verb. Exam Tip 📝 In SSC exams, questions based on modal verbs mainly test whether the candidate knows that modal verbs always take base form of the verb. If you eliminate options containing to + verb, -s/-es, or -ing, you will usually reach the correct answer quickly

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – Radhika has to lament the consequences of her friend’s actions.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Deplore. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Lament - दुःख व्यक्त करना - To express deep sorrow or regret Example: She lamented the loss of her loved one. Deplore - निंदा करना - To strongly disapprove or regret something Example: We deplore the violence that has been happening in the city. Fulfil - पूरा करना - To satisfy or meet a requirement or expectation Example: She fulfilled her dream by becoming a doctor. Decode - डिकोड करना - To convert coded data into a readable format Example: The cryptographer decoded the secret message. Curt - संक्षिप्त और असभ्य - Brief and abrupt in manner Example: He gave a curt reply to the question.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Derogatory
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Praising. Let’s know the meaning of all words: Derogatory - अपमानजनक - Showing a critical or disrespectful attitude Example: The politician's derogatory comments about his opponent's character sparked a heated debate during the election campaign. Praising - प्रशंसा करना - Expressing approval or admiration Example: The renowned art critic was praising the young artist's unique style and technique, predicting a bright future for her in the art world. Belittling - तुच्छ मानना - Making someone or something seem less important Example: The manager's belittling remarks about his team's efforts demotivated them and affected their productivity. Speck - छोटा कण - A small spot or particle Example: After thoroughly cleaning the room, she noticed a tiny speck of dust on the mirror that she had missed earlier. Brat - शरारती बच्चा - A badly behaved child Example: The spoiled brat threw a tantrum in the middle of the restaurant because his parents refused to buy him his favourite toy.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. Shiva recently ________ the armed forces, sending hundreds of officers into retirement.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Purged. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Quailed - डर जाना - To feel or show fear Example: She quailed at the thought of public speaking. Purged - शुद्ध करना/निकालना - To remove unwanted or undesirable people from an organization. Example: The government purged the ministry of corrupt officials. Junked - कबाड़ में डालना - To get rid of something as useless Example: The old car was junked after the accident. Disguised - छद्मवेश में - To change the appearance of something or someone Example: He disguised himself as a woman to avoid detection.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Grin from ear to ear
options: To shout or speak as loudly as possible
To smile very broadly, often as a result of being extremely pleased
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. To smile very broadly, often as a result of being extremely pleased. The idiom “grin from ear to ear” means to smile widely and continuously because of great happiness, excitement, or satisfaction. It suggests an expression of pure joy where the smile seems to stretch across the entire face. 📌 Origin of the Idiom: The phrase is based on a vivid physical image—a grin so wide that it appears to extend from one ear to the other. Over time, it came to describe someone who is extremely delighted. Example: When she heard about her selection, she was grinning from ear to ear. Hindi: “grin from ear to ear” = बहुत खुशी से मुस्कुराना / कानों तक मुस्कुराना

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: If he tried a little harder
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. If he tried a little harder. Replace “If he tried” with “If he had tried.” •The sentence expresses an unreal past condition—something that didn’t happen. In such cases, the third conditional should be used. • The third conditional structure is: “If + past perfect,” followed by “would have + past participle.” Therefore, the correct sentence should be: “If he had tried a little harder, he would have won the chess tournament last weekend.” It can be said that option 1 is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Maverick
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Malcontent. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Maverick - असंतुष्ट या अनोखा व्यक्ति - An independent individual who thinks and acts differently Example: Steve Jobs was a maverick in the tech industry, known for his innovative ideas and bold approach. Conservative - रूढ़िवादी - Resisting change or innovation Example: The conservative politician opposed the new law, citing concerns about its potential impact. Malcontent - असंतुष्ट - A person who is dissatisfied with the existing state of affairs Example: The malcontent employee was frustrated with the company's policies and lack of opportunities. Profusion - बहुतायत - A large or abundant amount of something Example: The garden was in profusion with colourful flowers and lush greenery. Sturdy - मजबूत - Strong and well-built Example: The sturdy bridge was designed to withstand extreme weather conditions.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- His steady progress in learning the language impressed the teacher.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Sporadic. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Steady - स्थिर - Consistent or regular in progress or movement Example: She made steady progress in her studies and graduated with Honors. Consistent - सुसंगत - Always behaving or performing in a similar way Example: The company's consistent quality of products earned it a loyal customer base. Stout - मजबूत - Strong and sturdy in build or structure Example: The stout wooden table withstood the heavy weight. Venal - भ्रष्ट - Corrupt or willing to bribe Example: The venal politician was accused of taking bribes from businessmen. Sporadic - छिटपुट - Occurring or done at irregular intervals Example: The sporadic power outages in the city caused inconvenience to the residents.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Despite the __________ warnings from management, the _____________ workers refused to comply with the new safety protocols.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Ominous, Recalcitrant. 📘 Explanation: The sentence describes management giving serious warnings, yet the workers refuse to comply with the new safety protocols, indicating a need for words expressing threat and stubborn disobedience. The other options contradict the meaning and tone. Let’s see the meaning of all the key words with examples: • Ominous – भयावह / खतरेकासंकेतदेनेवाला – Giving the impression that something bad or unpleasant is going to happen. Example: “The ominous clouds signalled a coming storm.” • Recalcitrant – हठी / अवज्ञाकारी – Stubbornly resistant to authority or control. Example: “The recalcitrant employee refused to obey company policies.” • Encouraging – प्रोत्साहितकरनेवाला – Giving support or confidence. Example: “Her encouraging words motivated the team.” • Cooperative – सहयोगी – Willing to work together. Example: “The cooperative students completed the project on time.” • Legitimate – वैध / उचित – Lawful or justified. Example: “She has a legitimate claim to the property.” • Nonchalant – उदासीन / बेपरवाह – Showing a casual lack of concern. Example: “He was nonchalant about the upcoming exam.” • Preventive – रोकथाम करने वाला – Intended to stop something from happening. Example: “Preventive measures were taken to avoid accidents.” • Obedient – आज्ञाकारी – Willing to comply with orders or rules. Example: “The obedient dog followed all commands.” ✅ So, Option [4] Ominous, Recalcitrant best fits the meaning and tone of the sentence.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Thing that causes somebody to lose their power, position, etc. and that cannot be avoided
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Nemesis. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Nemesis - विनाशक - A person or thing that causes someone to lose their power, position, or reputation Example: The company's financial struggles proved to be their nemesis, leading to eventual bankruptcy. Gangrene - गैंगरीन - A medical condition in which tissue dies due to lack of blood supply Example: The patient's wound became infected with gangrene, requiring amputation. Kudos - प्रशंसा - Praise or admiration for someone's achievement Example: The team received kudos for their outstanding performance in the competition. Pesky - परेशान करने वाला - Causing trouble or annoyance Example: The pesky mosquitoes kept biting us throughout the night.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Be hard up
options: To have a very good sale
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Having very little money. The idiom “be hard up” means to be short of money or in a financially difficult situation. It is commonly used to describe someone who is temporarily or continuously facing financial hardship. 📌Origin of the Idiom: The phrase developed from informal British usage, where “hard” suggests difficulty and “up” implies a blocked or constrained condition—together conveying the idea of being financially pressed. Example: After losing his job, he was hard up and had to cut down on expenses. Hindi: “be hard up” = पैसों की तंगी होना / आर्थिक रूप से परेशान होना

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. To have disturbed sleep/ brief sleep
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Cat nap. Let’s see the meaning of all words: A mare's nest - एक घोड़ी का घोंसला - A discovery that seems interesting but turns out to be worthless or false Example: The supposed breakthrough in the investigation turned out to be a mare's nest, leading to a dead end. Pass off - गुजर जाना - To pretend to be something or someone else, or to present something as something it is not Example: He tried to pass off the fake painting as an original, but it was soon discovered to be a forgery. Cat nap – झपकी - A short sleep or rest, especially during the day Example: After lunch, he decided to take a cat nap on the couch before resuming his work. Come to grief - असफल होना - To experience failure or misfortune Example: The company came to grief after making a series of poor investments.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. As a result, public trust in institutions gradually weakens over time. B. This erosion often begins when accountability mechanisms fail to function effectively. C. Democratic systems rely heavily on transparency to maintain legitimacy. D. Without transparent processes, misuse of power becomes easier to conceal.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. CBDA. 👉 Explanation: • C is the best opening sentence because it states the fundamental principle about democratic systems and transparency. • B cannot start because “This erosion” refers to a previously mentioned idea, which is introduced only after C. • D logically follows C, explaining the consequence of lacking transparency. • B then fits as it explains how the erosion mentioned earlier actually begins. • A is the concluding sentence since “As a result” summarizes the final impact on public trust. ✅ Final paragraph: Democratic systems rely heavily on transparency to maintain legitimacy. Without transparent processes, misuse of power becomes easier to conceal. This erosion often begins when accountability mechanisms fail to function effectively. As a result, public trust in institutions gradually weakens over time. 💡 Exam Tip (SSC CGL Hard Level): In difficult PQRS questions, focus on abstract nouns + reference words (this, such, these, as a result, therefore). They usually cannot open a paragraph and help you eliminate wrong sequences quickly.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Did the manager submit the quarterly report before the meeting started?
options: Was the quarterly report submitted by the manager before the meeting started?
Did the quarterly report get submitted before the meeting had started by the manager?
Was the quarterly report being submitted by the manager before the meeting started?
Is the quarterly report submitted by the manager before the meeting started?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Was the quarterly report submitted by the manager before the meeting started?. In interrogative passive transformation: • Active Structure: Did + Subject + V1 + Object + extra info...? → Did the manager submit the quarterly report before the meeting started? • Passive Structure: Was/Were + Object + V3 + by Subject + extra info...? → Was the quarterly report submitted by the manager before the meeting started? So: Passive: Was the quarterly report submitted by the manager before the meeting started?

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect/direct speech. He said to me, “You would better finish your homework before going out.”
options: He said that I should finish my homework before going out.
He told me that I had better finish my homework before going out.
He advised me to finish my homework before going out.
He said that I would better finish my homework before going out.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. He said that I would better finish my homework before going out. Change of Tense: • The reporting verb “said” is in the past tense. • The phrase “would better” does not change in reported speech; it remains the same because it's already a modal advising phrase. That: • The conjunction “that” introduces the indirect speech clause. Word Order: • The sentence is converted into a statement, keeping “would better” unchanged. Correct structure: He said that I would better finish my homework before going out.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. pressing. 💢 Explanation - The sentence emphasizes the urgency of ethical concerns arising from new technologies. Pressing means urgent and requiring immediate attention, which accurately captures the seriousness of these ethical questions. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - →Option 1 – trivial means unimportant, which contradicts the gravity of ethical issues. →Option 3 – incidental implies minor or secondary, weakening the seriousness. →Option 4 – obsolete means outdated, which does not fit future-oriented ethical debates.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. exacerbate. 💢 Explanation - Exacerbate means to make a problem worse. The passage warns that misuse of advanced technologies could deepen social divisions rather than reduce them, making "exacerbate" the most accurate choice. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - →Option 1 – bridge means reduce or connect gaps, opposite of intended meaning. →Option 2 – blur means make unclear, not worsen. →Option 4 – reconcile means restore harmony, which contradicts the warning tone.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. outpaces. 💢 Explanation - The sentence states: “the pace of discovery often ____ society’s capacity to adapt.” Here, the focus is on a speed imbalance between two processes—scientific discovery and society’s ability to adjust. Scientific breakthroughs are occurring at a very rapid rate, whereas social, legal, and institutional adaptation usually happens more slowly. Outpaces means “moves faster than” or “surpasses in speed,” which precisely captures this contrast. It conveys the idea that innovation is advancing more quickly than society can absorb, regulate, and respond to it, creating policy and governance challenges. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - →Option 1 – mirrors means reflects or matches. If discovery mirrored society’s capacity to adapt, both would move at the same speed, which contradicts the sentence’s emphasis on struggle and lag. → Option 3 – regulates means controls or governs. Scientific discovery does not control society’s capacity to adapt; rather, the problem is that adaptation cannot keep up with discovery. →Option 4 – synchronizes means moves in coordination or harmony. The passage highlights mismatch and imbalance, not alignment.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. complemented. 💢 Explanation - The passage argues that scientific progress should be supported by governance and public engagement. Complemented means accompanied or completed by something additional, fitting the cooperative relationship. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - →Option 2 – postponed means delayed, illogical here. →Option 3 – restricted means limited, which contradicts progress. →Option 4 – diluted means weakened, not supported.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. against. 💢 Explanation - The sentence reads: “Only then can societies fully harness innovation while guarding ____ unintended consequences.” The fixed collocation is guard against, which means to protect from, prevent, or take precautions to avoid something harmful. In the context of scientific advancement, societies want to benefit from innovation but simultaneously protect themselves from negative side effects such as misuse, ethical violations, or unforeseen harm. Therefore, against completes the standard and meaningful phrasal construction. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - →Option 2 – through does not form any accepted idiomatic expression with guard. Guard through has no established meaning in English. →Option 3 – with is grammatically incorrect in this structure and does not convey prevention. →Option 4 – upon is used in expressions like act upon or depend upon, but not with guard. Hence, it fails idiomatically and contextually.
